name: Octopus Pilaf (Oktapodi Pilaffi)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pound the octopus to tenderize it and cut into cubes.
2. In a large pan, melt 1/4 lb butter over medium heat. Add onions and sauté until golden brown.
3. Add garlic, bay leaf, oregano, fines herbes, and octopus to the pan. Sauté for a few minutes.
4. Stir in tomato paste, tomatoes, and wine. Mix well.
5. Cover and simmer on low heat for 1 hour or until the octopus is tender.
6. In a saucepan, combine 3-1/2 cups water, half of the octopus cooking liquid, and 2 tbsp butter. Bring to a boil.
7. Add rice to the pan and stir gently. Lower the heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
8. Serve by shaping the rice into individual mounds using a cup and topping with octopus and remaining sauce. This can be prepared in advance and reheated in the oven before serving. It pairs well with boiled greens and white retsina.
